summary: Typed runtime-boundary ledger for detecting architecture drift in Forge itself.
purpose: Make architecture constraints machine-readable instead of prose-only.
Give future AI implementation runs a durable contract artifact to read, review, and preserve.
Drive dedicated self-check workflows that detect convenience regressions before they become accepted behavior.

dispatch_readiness_states.description: A dispatch is derivable from the frozen spec and replayed state, but no prepare event has been appended yet.
dispatch_readiness_states.observer_requirement: Status and progress surfaces must describe this as projected_only and direct the operator to run `forge run next`.

dispatch_readiness_states.description: A pending dispatch or pending notification has been durably recorded in events.ndjson.
dispatch_readiness_states.observer_requirement: Execution surfaces may advertise this as ready for exec-dispatch.

dispatch_readiness_states.description: A prepared dispatch has been launched and has a recorded execution state.
dispatch_readiness_states.observer_requirement: Observers must render execution progress rather than readiness.

boundary_layers.name: preparation
allowed_operations: create required directories and input files

contracts.summary: Dispatch projection must not read prompt inputs or materialize prompt files.

anti_signals: show-progress fails because an input artifact body is unreadable

fix_guidance: Keep project_pending_dispatch limited to ids, commands, env, input paths, and output paths.
Move prompt assembly and file writes into explicit materialization helpers called only from preparation paths.

contracts.summary: exec-dispatch must only launch a persisted pending dispatch.

anti_signals: exec-dispatch appends DispatchPrepared or NotificationPrepared on its own

fix_guidance: Resolve exec-dispatch from replayed persisted state only.
Require `forge run next` or `forge run auto` to persist prepare events before execution.

contracts.summary: Observer surfaces must distinguish projected-only readiness from prepared readiness.

signals: status or progress claims a dispatch is ready to execute while exec-dispatch would still return no_dispatch
observer output lacks a readiness field or preparation hint

fix_guidance: Render readiness as typed state: projected_only, prepared, executing, terminal.
When projected_only, include the operator step required to persist the prepare event.

contracts.summary: Execution events must not bypass the prepare lifecycle.

signals: DispatchStarted exists without an earlier prepare event for the same dispatch id
NotificationDelivered or DispatchCompleted appears without the corresponding pending record

fix_guidance: Preserve parent-child ordering through explicit event append helpers.
Keep reducer invariants strict so malformed histories fail loudly.
